title: Using unsafe code blocks is security-sensitive
description: Using unsafe code blocks is security-sensitiveAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalsecuritySecurity HotspotUsingunsafecode blocks can lead to unintended security or stability risks.unsafecode blocks allow developers to use features such as pointers, fixed buffers, function calls through pointers and manual memory management. Such features may be necessary for interoperability with native libraries, as these often require pointers. It may also increase performance in some critical areas, as certain bounds checks are not executed in an unsafe context.unsafecode blocks aren’t necessarily dangerous, however, the contents of such blocks are not verified by the Common Language Runtime.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects usage of unsafe keyword in methods, classes, or structs

checks.message: Unsafe code block detected - review for security implications
Fixed buffer detected - unsafe code pattern
stackalloc without Span detected - consider using Span<T>

logic.triggering_conditions: Usage of unsafe keyword, fixed statement, or stackalloc without Span
logic.reasoning: Unsafe code blocks bypass CLR verification and can lead to memory corruption, buffer overflows, and security vulnerabilities. While sometimes necessary for interop or performance, they require careful review.
